title: [Docs]: Add Kubernetes deployment examples
problem: Kubernetes users need examples for running nats-sinks with configuration, secrets, readiness checks, graceful shutdown, resource limits, and observability separation. The current deployment guidance focuses on local and systemd operation.
proposal: Add Kubernetes examples for the sink worker and optional observability service. The examples should show ConfigMaps for non-secret JSON configuration, Secret references for credentials, mounted certificate or wallet material when needed, resource limits, termination grace periods, service accounts, and optional Prometheus textfile or future endpoint integration.
users: Platform teams deploying nats-sinks in Kubernetes, security reviewers checking least privilege, and maintainers supporting production deployments.
delivery_semantics: The examples must preserve graceful shutdown and commit-then-ACK behavior. Pods should stop accepting new work before termination and allow in-flight batches to finish or time out according to configuration.
security: Examples must use fake names only, avoid real endpoints or credentials, run with least privilege, and document secret handling without embedding private material. Network and filesystem permissions should be narrow.
acceptance: Kubernetes manifests or templates are added as examples.
Examples separate sink worker and observability concerns.
Resource limits, security context, and graceful shutdown settings are documented.
Examples avoid secrets and private operational values.
Docs explain which values users must customize.
tests: Run YAML or manifest linting if tooling is introduced, plus mkdocs builds and scripts/check.sh. Do not require a live cluster in unit or CI checks.
documentation: Update docs/operations.md, docs/security.md, docs/observability.md, README.md, examples, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close after the release containing the examples is published. Include release tag, files added, checks run, and follow-up work such as Helm chart support.
